title: Wide Character Packing Code (W)
text: Wide Character Packing Code (W) The W (wide character) packing code is used for Unicode characters. W n can contain n characters regardless how many bytes are needed for the characters. Some databases support only Unicode BMP (Basic Multilingual Plane ), or do not support Unicode at all. To control the behavior of the W packing code in such circumstances, you can use the Wide Character Behavior property of the entity interface or the assignment setting $WIDE_CHAR_BEHAVIOR . Note: Setting the assignment setting or property to use the character set, switches the W packing code to a C packing code, in which case you must also set the $DEF_CHARSET assignment setting or Character Set entity interface property. Related Links Packing Codes for Unicode By default, the W packing code is for Unicode characters. The U* packing code can also be used for holding Unicode characters. If the entity character set is Unicode, the C packing code can also be used for Unicode characters. Character Packing Code (C) The C packing code is used for String data types. Packing Codes for Other Data String, Image, and Raw data can be mapped to a variety of packing codes, including W for Unicode characters. Wide Character Behavior Specify the character set used for String fields with the W packing code. If the database does not support Unicode, or it supports only Unicode BMP, use this setting to change its behavior. Valid values are Unicode, BMP, or Use Character Set. $WIDE_CHAR_BEHAVIOR Specify the character set used for String fields with the W packing code. $DEF_CHARSET Specify the default character set used for storing data in String fields with C packing code.
